proof_trace: Memory allocated via calloc() at line 305 is not freed when mutex_trylock fails at line 310, causing the function to return NULL without cleanup in the else block (lines 312-314). Proof failed: no free(iter) call exists in the failure path.
attack_scenario: Any caller invoking assoc_get_iterator() while maintenance_lock is held will trigger mutex_trylock failure, causing memory leak of sizeof(struct assoc_iterator) bytes per failed call.

proof_trace: Reference leak in error path - do_item_remove(it) only called when it->refcount == 1, but the matching do_item_get() call requires unconditional cleanup regardless of refcount value. All other functions in codebase call do_item_remove() unconditionally to release their reference.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers delta operations on unlinked items with refcount > 1 repeatedly, causing memory references to accumulate and leak, eventually leading to denial of service through memory exhaustion.
reason: Maintainer (dormando) confirmed: refcount can only be 1 or 0 in this branch. do_item_get() increments to 1 on unlinked item. The if(refcount==1) guard is correct defensive programming — unconditional remove would underflow refcount to -1 when 0. PR #1282 rejected.

proof_trace: subopts_orig allocated via strdup() at L5179 but dozens of return statements between L5195-5603 exit before free() at L5611. Any sub-option validation failure leaks the allocated memory.
attack_scenario: Attacker provides invalid sub-option values (e.g. hashpower=1, slab_automove=999) causing main() to return early and leak strdup() allocated memory.

proof_trace: Memory allocated at L6089 is never freed when portnumber_file is NULL and either server_sockets() call fails (L6113 or L6132 exit paths), bypassing cleanup code at L6139-6140.
attack_scenario: Set MEMCACHED_PORT_FILENAME to trigger allocation, cause fopen() failure (portnumber_file=NULL), then trigger socket creation failure to hit exit() without cleanup.

proof_trace: Memory allocated via calloc() at line 432 is never freed on any return path. Function has two exit paths (break at L449, normal end at L463) but neither calls free(st.page_data), violating the API contract and causing a memory leak.
attack_scenario: Repeated calls to _evict_page() will continuously allocate memory without freeing it, leading to memory exhaustion and potential denial of service.

proof_trace: Memory leak occurs when item has unexpected refcount state: allocated item 'it' from do_item_get() is only freed if refcount==1, leaving unfreed references when refcount!=1 on error path.
attack_scenario: Race condition where item becomes unlinked with refcount>1 between retrieval and validation, causing accumulated memory leak leading to service degradation.
reason: Duplicate of PROP-M2b-mlk-003 — same bug different description。メンテナ(@dormando)がPR #1282で「この変更はバグを作る」と拒否。refcountはこの分岐で1か0のみ、無条件removeはrefcount underflowを招く。FP確定。

proof_trace: Multiple allocations (flist, db, db->buf, db->fname) leak on error paths. Function returns -1 without cleanup when allocations fail at lines 419-422 or 426-429, leaving previously allocated memory unreachable.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers allocation failures during proxy reconfiguration through resource exhaustion, causing cumulative memory leaks that worsen with each failed restart attempt.

proof_trace: getline() allocates memory for local 'line' variable (L154), but RESTART_NOTAG path (L175) and EOF error path (L210) return without freeing this memory, causing definite memory leaks in error conditions.
attack_scenario: Attacker provides malformed metadata files with invalid tags to trigger repeated RESTART_NOTAG conditions, causing memory leaks that accumulate to memory exhaustion.

proof_trace: Mutex lock acquired in cache_free() is never released when do_cache_free() returns early on redzone validation failures (lines 121, 128). The unlock at line 111 is bypassed, causing resource leak.
attack_scenario: Attacker provides corrupted memory to cache_free(), triggering redzone validation failure and early return, leaving mutex locked and causing deadlock on subsequent cache operations.
reason: proof_trace incorrect: do_cache_free() returns to cache_free() where pthread_mutex_unlock() is always called. Mutex is NOT leaked. The claim that unlock is bypassed is factually wrong — C function return goes back to caller.
